pm_decision_extract.reasoning_summary: BTOG exhibits a classic surge-short setup: intraday gap from 8.40 (prior close) to 12.52 (open), followed by −42% intraday collapse to 7.27 EOD and −36% 5d reversal. Fundamentals are severely distressed: zero revenue, −5.61M net income, negative equity (−1.13M), and high leverage (5.7M debt vs 4.0M assets). No news, SEC filings, or sentiment corroborate a catalyst narrative within the decision window. Wikipedia pageviews on CEO show baseline 0–7 views (no abnormal spike). Information integrity assessment flags T3-only sourcing, uncorroborated status, and fail-closed use_as_primary_signal_allowed=false. Upstream agents: narrative_event verdict='unsupported_claim' (no identifiable catalyst), alt_data_verify verdict='narrative_contradicted' (structural absence of expected corroboration), surge_short verdict='short' (defensible on bear grounds), risk verdict=no hard veto. The structural absence of news, filings, sentiment, and developer activity relative to the magnitude of the price collapse is itself bear-thesis evidence per §5.17 surge-short integrity-veto exception. The surge appears to be momentum/technical rather than value-driven. Macro regime (strict_quality_and_valuation_caution) is appropriate given BTOG's fundamental weakness. Evidence sufficiency is PARTIAL due to 20 dropped news items and missing filing confirmation, but the bear thesis is coherent on structural-absence grounds. Recommend SHORT entry at 0.5% (§4.8 fixed entry size) with WATCH discipline pending narrative confirmation. Invalidation: if SEC 8-K + alt-data corroborate a legitimate catalyst, or if fundamentals improve materially.
invalidation_conditions: If SEC 8-K or press release filed on or before 2025-04-08 reveals a legitimate M&A, partnership, product launch, or financing that is corroborated by alt-data (GitHub activity spike, sentiment reversal, institutional buying), the short thesis is invalidated.
If fundamental_snapshot is updated to show material revenue or positive EBITDA in next quarterly filing, short thesis weakens materially.
If alternative_data_features (when wired) shows abnormal retail attention, developer activity, or institutional positioning supporting a bull narrative, short thesis is challenged.
If macro_regime shifts to risk-on and equity_discipline relaxes, valuation floor may rise and reduce short urgency.
If BTOG announces a reverse split or delisting notice post-decision, the short thesis may be superseded by structural corporate action.

BTOG exhibits classic structural absence: zero revenue, negative equity, no news corroboration, no SEC filings, no sentiment/analyst coverage, no developer activity. However, the evidence packet has 20 news items dropped post-cutoff (unknown content) and filing_confirmation not_evaluated (8-K adapter not wired). This creates a gap: the bear thesis is defensible on absence grounds, but the missing narrative blocks prevent definitive HIGH confidence. The PM decision is SHORT at 0.5% entry with WATCH discipline pending narrative confirmation. This is appropriate per §13.6 soft-reasoning framework: the preponderance of evidence (price collapse, negative fundamentals, structural absence of corroboration) favors the bear thesis, even though some narrative blocks are missing.

narrative_event.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The evidence packet architecture correctly enforces PIT discipline by filtering post-cutoff news (20 items dropped). However, this creates a structural blind spot: if the catalyst occurred on 2025-04-08 morning (within the opening window), it will be in the dropped items and invisible to the agent. The agent cannot reason about post-cutoff data, so the absence of a catalyst in the pre-cutoff window is genuine, not a data gap. This is correct per PIT rules but limits the agent's ability to explain intraday price moves in opening-window mode. Recommendation: For opening-window decisions, consider a 'post-cutoff news summary' field (for display only, not decision input) to flag whether material news occurred after cutoff.

alt_data_verify.reasoning_summary: BTOG exhibits severe price deterioration (−36.2% in 5d, −74.5% in 20d, −85.7% in 60d) with volume collapse (31.2% of 20d average). Fundamental snapshot shows revenue=0, net_income=−$5.61M, total_equity=−$1.13M (negative book value), and total_debt=$5.7M — indicating a distressed, pre-revenue or failed venture. The macro regime is 'Overheat/Expansionary' with strict equity discipline, which is appropriate given the company's fundamental weakness. Alternative-data evidence is sparse: Wikipedia pageviews for 'Sam_Ahmedzai' (CEO/founder) show minimal, erratic traffic (peak 7 views on 2025-03-20, mostly 0–3 views), with no sustained attention spike. No news items exist within the cutoff window (20 items dropped post-cutoff). GitHub adapter failed (unmapped_ticker). FMP sentiment endpoints returned zero rows. Polygon news returned zero rows. Information-integrity assessment flags T1=0, T2=0, T3=1, T4=0 — only one T3 source tier present, and use_as_primary_signal_allowed=false per schema v1.1 fail-closed policy. The absence of corroborating alt-data (news, GitHub activity, sentiment, filings) relative to the magnitude of the price collapse is itself informative: a legitimate company experiencing a 75%+ drawdown would typically generate news coverage, regulatory filings, or social attention. The structural absence of these signals, combined with zero revenue and negative equity, suggests the narrative (if any exists) is either not being publicly articulated or is not reaching mainstream sources. The Wikipedia pageview data on the CEO is too sparse and low-traffic to constitute meaningful corroboration of any bull thesis. No positive catalyst or reversal signal is present in the alt-data.

If GitHub activity or developer ecosystem signals emerge showing active product development
If institutional ownership or insider buying is disclosed in subsequent 13F or Form 4 filings
If revenue or profitability metrics reverse in next quarterly filing

alt_data_verify.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The information_integrity_assessment block is marked 'insufficient_evidence' with use_as_primary_signal_allowed=false, which is a fail-closed stance appropriate for v1. However, this prevents the agent from reasoning about T3-only evidence in a nuanced way. A pollution-defense layer (mentioned in schema v1.1 decision #8 as 'still unimplemented') would allow conditional use of T3 evidence when corroborated by hard-to-fake alt-data (e.g., SEC filings, GitHub activity). In this case, the absence of such corroboration is itself the signal, but the schema does not yet formalize that reasoning path.

Assess whether the 1:30 stock split (2023-05-30) was a reverse split (likely, given current price and negative equity); if so, this is a distress signal
alt_data_verify.narrative_price_gap_rationale: BTOG's 85.7% 60-day price collapse is extreme and suggests a severe negative catalyst or fundamental deterioration. However, no corresponding news, regulatory filing, or social attention is present in the alt-data. The structural absence of corroborating evidence (news, GitHub activity, sentiment, SEC filings) relative to the magnitude of the price move is itself bear-thesis evidence: a legitimate company experiencing a 75%+ drawdown would typically generate detectable signals. The Wikipedia pageview data on the CEO is minimal and erratic, showing no sustained attention spike. The fundamental snapshot reveals zero revenue, negative equity, and high leverage — consistent with a failed venture or pre-revenue company in distress. The lack of any positive narrative or catalyst in the alt-data, combined with the extreme price deterioration and negative fundamentals, suggests the market is pricing in a severe risk (bankruptcy, dilution, or business failure) that is not being publicly articulated or is not reaching mainstream sources.

alt_data_verify.hard_to_fake_signal_summary: Fundamental snapshot (SEC-filed, PIT-safe): revenue=0, net_income=−$5.61M, total_equity=−$1.13M, total_debt=$5.7M. Price snapshot (market data): −85.7% in 60d, volume collapse to 31% of 20d avg. Alternative-data absence: no news (20 items dropped post-cutoff), no GitHub activity (unmapped), no sentiment (endpoints empty), no SEC filings (not_connected). CEO Wikipedia pageviews: minimal, erratic (peak 7 views, mostly 0–3), no sustained spike. These are hard-to-fake signals of a distressed, pre-revenue company with no public narrative or catalyst.

surge_short.reasoning_summary: BTOG surged intraday from 8.40 (prior close) to 12.52 (open) on 2025-04-08, then collapsed to 7.27 EOD (−42% intraday, −36% 5d). Fundamental snapshot shows zero revenue, −5.61M net income, negative equity (−1.13M), and distressed valuation (negative PE, EV/EBITDA). No news items, SEC filings, or press releases corroborate a catalyst narrative within the decision window. Wikipedia pageviews on CEO 'Sam_Ahmedzai' show baseline 0–7 views (no spike). Information integrity assessment flags T3-only sourcing, uncorroborated status, and fail-closed use_as_primary_signal_allowed=false. The surge appears to be momentum/technical rather than value-driven. Structural absence of expected corroboration (news, filings, sentiment, developer activity) relative to the public narrative is consistent with a bear thesis: the surge is a momentum artefact, not real value creation. However, evidence sufficiency is PARTIAL because (1) news_event_summary has 20 items dropped post-cutoff (unknown content), (2) filing_confirmation is not_evaluated (8-K adapter not wired), and (3) narrative_price_gap_assessment is pending. The bear thesis is defensible on structural-absence grounds, but the missing narrative blocks prevent HIGH confidence. Recommend SHORT with WATCH discipline pending narrative confirmation.
invalidation_conditions: If SEC 8-K or press release filed post-cutoff reveals a legitimate M&A, partnership, or product launch that explains the surge and is corroborated by alt-data (e.g., GitHub activity spike, sentiment reversal, institutional buying), the short thesis is invalidated.
If fundamental_snapshot is updated to show material revenue or positive EBITDA in next quarter, short thesis weakens.
If alternative_data_features (when wired) shows abnormal retail attention, developer activity, or institutional positioning supporting the narrative, short thesis is challenged.
If macro_regime shifts to risk-on and equity_discipline relaxes, valuation floor may rise, reducing short urgency.

risk.decision_or_assessment: Hard-veto evaluation per §5.13 for surge_short candidate BTOG
risk.reasoning_summary: Mechanical evaluation of §5.13 hard-veto conditions (a)–(e) for candidate_type=surge_short. Condition (a): sleeve cap breach — not applicable; surge_short is not a long entry. Condition (b): short entry violates §4.8 caps — no current sleeve position data provided; cannot evaluate. Condition (c): critical data_quality_flags — examined; no critical-severity flags present. Condition (d): anti-pollution integrity veto — RELAXED per §5.17 for surge_short; INAPPLICABLE to candidate_type=surge_short per §5.13(d) relaxation 2026-05-06. Condition (e): lookahead veto — envelope.data_after_cutoff_used=false AND envelope.lookahead_safe=true; NOT tripped. No hard veto conditions are tripped.
invalidation_conditions: If current surge_short sleeve position + proposed 0.5% position exceeds 10% sleeve cap, condition (b) trips.
If any data_quality_flags block is later found with severity=critical, condition (c) trips retroactively.
If envelope.data_after_cutoff_used is later corrected to true, condition (e) trips retroactively.

pm.reasoning_summary: BTOG exhibits a classic surge-short setup: intraday gap from 8.40 (prior close) to 12.52 (open), followed by −42% intraday collapse to 7.27 EOD and −36% 5d reversal. Fundamentals are severely distressed: zero revenue, −5.61M net income, negative equity (−1.13M), and high leverage (5.7M debt vs 4.0M assets). No news, SEC filings, or sentiment corroborate a catalyst narrative within the decision window. Wikipedia pageviews on CEO show baseline 0–7 views (no abnormal spike). Information integrity assessment flags T3-only sourcing, uncorroborated status, and fail-closed use_as_primary_signal_allowed=false. Upstream agents: narrative_event verdict='unsupported_claim' (no identifiable catalyst), alt_data_verify verdict='narrative_contradicted' (structural absence of expected corroboration), surge_short verdict='short' (defensible on bear grounds), risk verdict=no hard veto. The structural absence of news, filings, sentiment, and developer activity relative to the magnitude of the price collapse is itself bear-thesis evidence per §5.17 surge-short integrity-veto exception. The surge appears to be momentum/technical rather than value-driven. Macro regime (strict_quality_and_valuation_caution) is appropriate given BTOG's fundamental weakness. Evidence sufficiency is PARTIAL due to 20 dropped news items and missing filing confirmation, but the bear thesis is coherent on structural-absence grounds. Recommend SHORT entry at 0.5% (§4.8 fixed entry size) with WATCH discipline pending narrative confirmation. Invalidation: if SEC 8-K + alt-data corroborate a legitimate catalyst, or if fundamentals improve materially.
invalidation_conditions: If SEC 8-K or press release filed on or before 2025-04-08 reveals a legitimate M&A, partnership, product launch, or financing that is corroborated by alt-data (GitHub activity spike, sentiment reversal, institutional buying), the short thesis is invalidated.
If fundamental_snapshot is updated to show material revenue or positive EBITDA in next quarterly filing, short thesis weakens materially.
If alternative_data_features (when wired) shows abnormal retail attention, developer activity, or institutional positioning supporting a bull narrative, short thesis is challenged.
If macro_regime shifts to risk-on and equity_discipline relaxes, valuation floor may rise and reduce short urgency.
If BTOG announces a reverse split or delisting notice post-decision, the short thesis may be superseded by structural corporate action.

decision_log: 2025-04-08T09:30:00-04:00 [ENVELOPE] Decision timestamp locked; PIT discipline enforced; lookahead_safe=true, data_after_cutoff_used=false.
